skill.name: imagegen-frontend-mobile
skill.description: Generates premium mobile app screen images and multi-screen flow concepts for iOS, Android, and cross-platform products. It focuses on readable hierarchy, platform-aware structure, consistent device mockups, controlled palettes, art-directed imagery, and believable app navigation. The skill is for image generation only and does not produce implementation code.
skill.summary: Generate polished mobile app screen images and flow mockups with strong hierarchy, readable text, and consistent product direction.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, sensitive data, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found markdown-only design guidance. The backtick matches are inline scale descriptions, the SAM matches are ordinary section headings, and no executable code, network behavior, credential access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Scale Descriptions
low_findings.description: The external command findings are markdown inline code spans that describe numeric design scales. They do not execute Ruby, shell, or any other command, and no command injection path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are plain markdown text under the active baseline configuration. They contain no executable syntax outside documentation formatting.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive Data Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The critical SAM findings are section headings for safe-area and image-system rules. They do not reference the Windows SAM database or any credential store.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact locations are ordinary design-rule headings. The surrounding text discusses mobile layout and image use, not secrets or system files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design-Language Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts map to ordinary design words such as safe, system, mask, consistency, and app flow. No cryptographic APIs, file enumeration, network probing, or host discovery instructions were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: A broad semantic review found image-generation instructions only. The flagged terms are used in mobile design quality guidance, not security-sensitive operations.

content.value_statement: Mobile app image prompts often create generic screens with weak flow logic. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create polished, readable mobile app screen concepts.
actual_capabilities: Generates mobile app screen images for onboarding, auth, dashboards, profiles, settings, chat, commerce, and other flows.
Selects iOS, Android, or neutral cross-platform visual direction based on the request.
Creates multi-screen flows with consistent palette, typography mood, spacing, icon style, and device framing.
Prioritizes readable text, safe-area awareness, believable navigation, and logical screen progression.
Uses art-directed imagery, subtle texture, and controlled decorative assets when they improve the concept.
Frames screens in clean phone mockups by default while keeping the app content primary.
limitations: It generates images only and does not write SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, HTML, or other code.
It does not create production-ready design files, component libraries, or exported UI specifications.
It depends on the active image generation tool for final visual fidelity and text rendering accuracy.
It is optimized for mobile app concepts, not websites, desktop dashboards, or landing pages.

use_cases.title: Pitch a New App Concept
use_cases.description: Create a polished set of mobile screens that explains the product idea, core flow, and visual direction.

use_cases.title: Explore Product Redesign Directions
use_cases.description: Generate several premium mobile screen concepts with stronger hierarchy, imagery, palette control, and platform awareness.

use_cases.title: Prepare Visual References for a Team
use_cases.description: Produce readable app flow images that help align stakeholders before detailed design or implementation begins.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner App Concept
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a simple first visual direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a premium mobile app screen concept for a fitness tracking app. Show the main home screen in a clean phone mockup with readable text and calm spacing.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Screen Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the user journey matters.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 4-screen iOS-native onboarding flow for a social reading app. Keep the screens visually consistent, vary the composition, and use short readable copy.

prompt_templates.title: Category-Specific Redesign
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when improving an existing product direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign a premium mobile commerce app flow with browse, product detail, cart, and checkout screens. Use strong product imagery and clean checkout hierarchy.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Art Direction System
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a distinctive visual system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 6-screen cross-platform mobile app concept for a wellness marketplace. Use a soft tactile surface treatment, consistent image frames, custom-feeling icons, and a controlled non-generic palette.

output_examples.input: Generate a premium fitness app concept.
output_examples.output: A set of clean mobile app screen images showing onboarding, home metrics, activity detail, and profile views in consistent phone mockups.
Five app-native commerce screens with browse, product detail, cart, checkout, and confirmation states using consistent product imagery and clear hierarchy.

output_examples.input: Make an onboarding flow for a social app.
output_examples.output: Multiple onboarding screens with varied layouts, short copy, visible phone frames, readable typography, and one coherent visual system.

best_practices: Specify the app category, target platform, number of screens, and core user journey before generating images.
Ask for readable text, safe-area awareness, and consistent phone mockup framing when the output will be reviewed by stakeholders.
Request regeneration when a screen has tiny text, weak navigation logic, inconsistent styling, or generic visual direction.
anti_patterns: Do not ask this skill to write app code or implementation instructions.
Do not request one crowded collage when the product needs a clear multi-screen flow.
Do not use it for desktop dashboards, landing pages, or website hero sections.

faq.question: Does this skill generate code?
faq.answer: No. It generates mobile app screen images and flow images only.

faq.question: Can it create both iOS and Android concepts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can bias the visual direction toward iOS-native, Android-native, or cross-platform mobile patterns.

faq.question: Does it support multi-screen app flows?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to generate enough screens for believable onboarding, auth, commerce, dashboard, and other mobile flows.

faq.question: Will the screens appear inside phone mockups?
faq.answer: By default, yes. The skill asks for clean phone framing while keeping the app content as the main focus.

faq.question: Can it improve generic AI app mockups?
faq.answer: Yes. It explicitly pushes for stronger hierarchy, controlled palettes, readable text, better imagery, and consistent screen systems.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production UI handoff?
faq.answer: No. It is best for visual exploration, concept presentation, and design direction before detailed production design.
